content:
# Luke Chapter 10 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

---

## Summary

Luke 10 contains 42 verses covering four major sections: the Sending of the Seventy-two (vv. 1-16), the Return of the Seventy-two (vv. 17-24), the Parable of the Good Samaritan (vv. 25-37), and Martha and Mary (vv. 38-42). The three translators showed strong agreement on the overall structure and most key theological terms. Disagreements centered on English register, participial rendering, degree of interpretive expansion, and vocabulary precision. SOPHIA sided with LOGOS in the majority of decisions (approximately 22 of 32 contested points), with EMET prevailing in 5 cases and PNEUMA in none independently. The remaining cases involved SOPHIA blending elements from multiple translators.

---

## Detailed Disagreements

| Verse | Issue | EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic) | SOPHIA Decision | Rationale |
|-------|-------|----------------|----------------|-------------------|-----------------|-----------|
| 10:2 | "plentiful" vs. "much" | "much" | "plentiful" | "plentiful" | "plentiful" | 2-to-1; natural collocate for "harvest" |
| 10:2 | "laborers" vs. "workers" | "workers" | "laborers" | "workers" | "laborers" | Traditional register for harvest imagery; *ergatai* |
| 10:2 | "beseech" vs. "ask" | "ask" | "beseech" | "Ask" | "beseech" | *deomai* is stronger than simple request; urgency preserved |
| 10:4 | "road" vs. "way" | "way" | "road" | "road" | "road" | 2-to-1; more concrete for *hodos* in travel context |
| 10:6 | "is there" vs. "be there" vs. "lives there" | "is there" | "be there" | "lives there" | "is there" | EMET's present indicative is faithful; LOGOS's subjunctive unnecessary |
| 10:7 | "from them" vs. "provided by them" | "the things from them" | "the things provided by them" | "whatever they provide" | "the things provided by them" | LOGOS clarifies elliptical *ta par auton* naturally |
| 10:10 | "having gone out" vs. "go out" | "having gone out... say" | "go out... and say" | "go out... and say" | "go out... and say" | 2-to-1; imperative flow is clearer |
| 10:11 | Dust clause structure | Greek word order preserved | Rearranged for clarity | Simplified | LOGOS rearrangement | Readable English preserving the vivid image |
| 10:13 | "Woe" vs. "How terrible" | "Woe to you" | "Woe to you" | "How terrible it will be for you" | "Woe to you" | 2-to-1; prophetic register preserved for *ouai* |
| 10:13 | "mighty works" vs. "miracles" | "mighty-works" | "mighty works" | "miracles" | "mighty works" | 2-to-1; *dynameis* = acts of power, not simply "miracles" |
| 10:15 | "Hades" vs. "realm of the dead" | "Hades" | "Hades" | "the realm of the dead" | "Hades" | 2-to-1; transliterate proper noun consistently |
| 10:18 | Satan's fall rendering | "as lightning, having fallen out of the heaven" | "having fallen like lightning from heaven" | "fall from heaven like a flash of lightning" | "like lightning, having fallen from heaven" | Preserves aorist *pesonta* (completed fall); "from" simplifies "out of" |
| 10:18 | "I was watching" vs. "I was beholding" | "I was watching" | "I was beholding" | "I watched" | "I was watching" | EMET and LOGOS preserve imperfect; "watching" less archaic than "beholding" |
| 10:21 | "infants" vs. "babes" | "infants" | "babes" | "those as open as little children" | "infants" | Most precise for *nepiois*; "babes" archaic; PNEUMA expands interpretively |
| 10:21 | "I praise" vs. "I confess-praise" | "I confess-praise to you" | "I praise you" | "I praise you" | "I praise you" | 2-to-1; hyphenated compound is not natural English |
| 10:25 | "lawyer" vs. "law-expert" | "law-expert" | "lawyer" | "legal expert" | "lawyer" | Established formal equivalent for *nomikos* |
| 10:25 | Question form | "having done what, shall I inherit" | "what shall I do to inherit" | "what must I do to inherit" | "what shall I do to inherit" | Natural English word order; LOGOS faithful to sense |
| 10:27 | Preposition for "heart" | "out of your whole heart" | "with all your heart" | "with all your heart" | "with all your heart" | 2-to-1; established Shema rendering in English |
| 10:30 | *hypolabon* rendering | "having taken this up" | "taking up the question" | "In reply" | "taking up the question" | LOGOS explains the nuanced verb *hypolabon* clearly |
| 10:30 | "inflicted wounds" vs. "laid on blows" | "having laid on blows" | "inflicted wounds" | "beat him" | "inflicted wounds" | Precise for *plēgas epithentes*; PNEUMA too simple |
| 10:33 | "journeying" vs. "traveling" | "traveling" | "journeying" | "as he traveled" | "journeying" | More formal register appropriate to parabolic narrative |
| 10:33 | Compassion rendering | "was moved-with-compassion" | "was moved with compassion" | "his heart went out to him" | "was moved with compassion" | 2-to-1; PNEUMA is interpretive paraphrase of *esplagchnisthe* |
| 10:34 | "beast" vs. "animal" vs. "donkey" | "his own animal" | "his own beast" | "his own donkey" | "his own beast" | Traditional rendering; *ktenos* is generic; PNEUMA specifies beyond Greek |
| 10:35 | "on the next day" vs. "on the morrow" | "on the next day" | "on the morrow" | "the next day" | "on the next day" | Clear without archaism; preserves prepositional structure |
| 10:36 | "seems to you" vs. "do you think" | "seems to you" | "do you think" | "do you think" | "do you think" | 2-to-1; natural English for *dokei soi* |
| 10:37 | Mercy description | "the one having done the mercy with him" | "the one who showed mercy to him" | "the one who showed him mercy" | "the one who showed mercy to him" | LOGOS is clear English for *poiesas to eleos met autou* |
| 10:37 | Final command | "Go, and you yourself do likewise" | "Go, and you do likewise" | "Go and do the same" | "Go, and you do likewise" | Preserves emphatic *su* and traditional "likewise" for *homoios* |
| 10:39 | "hearing" vs. "listening" | "was hearing his word" | "was listening to his word" | "listened to what he was saying" | "was listening to his word" | "Listening" conveys attentive reception; imperfect tense preserved |
| 10:40 | Distraction phrase | "distracted about much serving" | "distracted with much serving" | "pulled in every direction" | "distracted with much serving" | Natural English preposition; PNEUMA too expansive |
| 10:40 | Martha's approach | "having stood over him" | "having come to him" | "came to him" | "having come to him" | *epistasa* rendered as approach; avoids confrontational overtone |
| 10:41 | Introductory conjunction | "And having answered" | "But the Lord answered" | "But the Lord answered" | "But the Lord answered" | 2-to-1; adversative "But" captures the contrast |
| 10:42 | "good portion" vs. "good part" vs. "better part" | "the good part" | "the good portion" | "the better part" | "the good portion" | *merida* = "portion"; PNEUMA's "better" exceeds positive *agathen* |

---

## Notable Agreements (All Three Translators)

The following key renderings saw agreement across all three translators, requiring no arbitration:

- **10:1** — "seventy-two" (all three follow Sinaiticus ΕΒΔΟΜΗΚΟΝΤΑ ΔΥΟ)
- **10:1** — "appointed" for ΑΝΕΔΕΙΞΕΝ
- **10:5** — "Peace to this house"
- **10:6** — "son of peace" (Semitic idiom preserved by all)
- **10:9** — "The kingdom of God has drawn near"
- **10:12** — "more tolerable for Sodom"
- **10:16** — Chain of hearing/rejecting (all preserve the threefold structure)
- **10:17** — "even the demons are subject to us in your name"
- **10:19** — "serpents and scorpions" (all transliterate rather than interpret)
- **10:20** — "your names have been written in the heavens"
- **10:22** — Mutual Father-Son knowledge (all preserve reciprocal structure)
- **10:28** — "do this, and you shall live"
- **10:29** — "wishing to justify himself"
- **10:31** — "by coincidence" (all three render *kata synkyrian* similarly)
- **10:41** — "Martha, Martha" (doubled vocative preserved by all)
- **10:42** — "one thing is needful" (all follow Sinaiticus shorter reading)

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA, from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century CE).*
